title: Kagome Design · AI design partner for live products
full_text:
Now in private beta. Join the waitlist. Design features inside your live product
Kagome learns your product's design system on sight, then proposes polished new UI that already feels native, built on the same tokens, type, and components your team is already using.
No credit card. We’ll reach out when your slot opens up.
Chat Designs
Invite Team
Today
Updated the empty state with a softer welcome line and reused your icon set.
Make the first three entries more prominent
● ● ● Designing
Make the first three entries more prominent
Attach
KC
Kayla Chen
kayla@nimbusapp.com
https://www.nimbusapp.com/

Launch Day
By Nimbus Ventures
Have a say! Launches are competing for $30K in credits.
Voting ends in
0d : 15h : 25m
Vote now
Product A design partner that lives
where your product already lives
Forget round-trips between mockups, tickets, and another rewrite. Kagome works against your real product, with your design system already in hand.
Designs against your live product
Point at any screen and describe the change. Kagome reads the surrounding context (your layout, components, and styles) and proposes something that fits the page it's standing on.
✨ Make this empty state feel friendlier.
I'll add a short welcome line, a primary action that matches your button styles, and a soft illustration pulled from your icon set
Show diff Try a variation
Ask for another change
Fluent in your design system
Tokens, components, naming, spacing: the same conventions your team agreed on six retros ago. Every output uses what you've already built.
Using Button / primary from your system
Matched components
EmptyState / soft
• color/surface-2
• text/secondary
• radius/lg
Use this Show source
Multiple takes on every prompt
Kagome proposes a handful of variants for each request, with different layouts, copy, density, and tones, so you're choosing between good options instead of iterating away from a generic first draft.
3 VARIANTS READY
Loop the team in before it ships
Invite teammates to comment on any proposed change. PMs, designers, and engineers can suggest tweaks, react, and approve, all in the same place, before anything reaches main.
◐ 3 reviewers · 2 comments
Sarah PM
Can we add a back button to the modal?
Mike Eng
+1, and match the modal radius too.
Approved by Anna
2m ago
Wires up real behavior
Kagome doesn't stop at the visuals. Connect a new button to where it should go. Open an existing screen, jump into a fresh flow Kagome just designed, or trigger any action your prototype needs.
WIRING UP
Checkout button
onClick
NEW
PaymentModal
open(cart)
Hand off to Cursor, Claude Code, or Codex
When the design is ready, pull it into your codebase with the AI coding agent your team already uses. Kagome exports a clean spec with components, tokens, interactions, and copy, so the implementation lands without translation.
READY TO HAND OFF
Cursor
Open the spec in your editor
Claude Code
Ship the change from the CLI
Codex
Generate the implementation
How it works From idea to a real change
in your product, in minutes
Point Kagome at your product
Paste in your URL. Kagome loads your live product and pulls in the design system: colors, typography, spacing, and repeated components. Every session starts already fluent in your visual language.
Describe what you want to build
Type a prompt, sketch a layout, or point at a screen and explain the change. Kagome proposes a design that fits the surrounding context, not a generic template.
Review, iterate, and ship the design
Refine with the team, tweak in place, and approve in one spot. When the design is ready, export it, share a link, or hand it off to engineering. No code repository required.
Exploration
● ● ● Designing variants
Variant A
Light · Default
Select
Variant B
Soft Cream
Selected
Variant C
Editorial Dark
Select
Variant D
Editorial Light
Select
Try a few directions for the Nimbus homepage
10x Faster from idea to staging. Teams ship designs in the time it used to take to schedule a kickoff.
85% Of Kagome's suggestions are merged with little or no editing, because the output already matches the system.
0 Off-brand pixels. Every design Kagome proposes uses your existing tokens, components, and conventions.
Enterprise Built for teams that
still ship carefully
Design system fidelity
Tokens, components, type scale, and spacing. Kagome treats your system as the source of truth and refuses to drift.
Buttons / primary MATCHED
from your shared library
color/surface-2 TOKEN
token used in 14 places
✦ Suggestion REUSE
EmptyState / soft
already used on Inbox and Reports
Learns your product on sight
Point Kagome at any URL and it maps your tokens, type scale, and repeated components. No manual import, no setup, no migration.
ANALYZING PRODUCT
Review and approve
Every proposed design opens in a live review. Role-based approvals, comment threads, version history, and a clean undo for the day it's needed.
Side-by-side preview. Role-based review. Comment threads. Version history. Clean undo.
Features The shortest path from
“what if we tried…” to live
Everything you’d expect from a design tool, plus the parts that usually live in the engineering side of the house. Neatly bundled so neither team has to wait on the other.
Live product context
Kagome works against the running app, with your current layout, components, and styles, so every suggestion is grounded in what actually exists.
Design system matching
Pulls from your tokens, components, and conventions. The output looks like something your senior designer would have shipped.
Zero-setup import
Paste a URL and Kagome maps your design system in minutes: tokens, type scale, spacing, and repeated components, all without manual exports or library plugins.
Component reuse
Whenever there's already a component for the job, Kagome reaches for it first. New components only get created when none of the existing ones fit.
Preview, iterate, undo
Every proposed change opens as a side-by-side preview. Tweak it, branch a variant, undo cleanly. Only the design your team approves moves on.
Built for teams
PMs draft, designers refine, engineers review. Everyone meets at the same artifact: a real change, not a Figma frame or a Loom.
Testimonials What teams are saying
“ Our PMs are shipping landing-page experiments themselves now. The variants are on-brand, they pass review, and our designers finally have time for the bigger problems. ”
Sarah T.
Head of Product
“ I'd been skeptical of design AI until I saw it reuse a component from our internal library instead of inventing one. That moment sold me. It actually understands our design system. ”
Michael R.
Staff Engineer
“ I used to spend half my week walking engineering through Figma. Now I review a diff that already looks like our app. Less translation, more designing. ”
Johnny S.
Senior Product Designer
“ We replaced the back-and-forth on small UI work with a tight loop in Kagome. Tickets that used to take a week now close the same afternoon. ”
Jennifer L.
Engineering Manager
“ It feels less like prompting an AI and more like working with a teammate who already read every component file before joining the call. ”
David K.
Co-founder
“ Our PMs are shipping landing-page experiments themselves now. The variants are on-brand, they pass review, and our designers finally have time for the bigger problems. ”

how fast you want to move

Free

Unlimited sessions

Get started No credit card required.

For working teams shipping designs daily. Handoff and team review built in.

Unlimited sessions

FAQ Questions, answered.
How does Kagome learn my design system?
Do I need to connect Kagome to my codebase?
Is my product data private?
How does it stay on-brand?
What about review and approval?
Stop describing features.
Start building them.
Join the private beta and see how much of your product backlog Kagome can move on its own, without straying from your design system.

Product A design partner that lives
where your product already lives
Forget round-trips between mockups, tickets, and another rewrite. Kagome works against your real product, with your design system already in hand.
Designs against your live product
Point at any screen and describe the change. Kagome reads the surrounding context (your layout, components, and styles) and proposes something that fits the page it's standing on.

Multiple takes on every prompt
Kagome proposes a handful of variants for each request, with different layouts, copy, density, and tones, so you're choosing between good options instead of iterating away from a generic first draft.

Loop the team in before it ships

How it works From idea to a real change
in your product, in minutes
Point Kagome at your product
Paste in your URL. Kagome loads your live product and pulls in the design system: colors, typography, spacing, and repeated components. Every session starts already fluent in your visual language.
Describe what you want to build
Type a prompt, sketch a layout, or point at a screen and explain the change. Kagome proposes a design that fits the surrounding context, not a generic template.
Review, iterate, and ship the design
Refine with the team, tweak in place, and approve in one spot. When the design is ready, export it, share a link, or hand it off to engineering. No code repository required.

Learns your product on sight
Point Kagome at any URL and it maps your tokens, type scale, and repeated components. No manual import, no setup, no migration.
ANALYZING PRODUCT
Review and approve
Every proposed design opens in a live review. Role-based approvals, comment threads, version history, and a clean undo for the day it's needed.
Side-by-side preview. Role-based review. Comment threads. Version history. Clean undo.
Features The shortest path from
“what if we tried…” to live
Everything you’d expect from a design tool, plus the parts that usually live in the engineering side of the house. Neatly bundled so neither team has to wait on the other.
Live product context
Kagome works against the running app, with your current layout, components, and styles, so every suggestion is grounded in what actually exists.
Design system matching
Pulls from your tokens, components, and conventions. The output looks like something your senior designer would have shipped.
Zero-setup import
Paste a URL and Kagome maps your design system in minutes: tokens, type scale, spacing, and repeated components, all without manual exports or library plugins.
Component reuse
Whenever there's already a component for the job, Kagome reaches for it first. New components only get created when none of the existing ones fit.
Preview, iterate, undo
Every proposed change opens as a side-by-side preview. Tweak it, branch a variant, undo cleanly. Only the design your team approves moves on.
Built for teams
PMs draft, designers refine, engineers review. Everyone meets at the same artifact: a real change, not a Figma frame or a Loom.
